frontier: Smallest N that is the sum of three prime cubes in exactly 6 representations (OEIS A385316 a(6)), with current lower bound a(6) > 4.99e11.

Search to C=1e9: Certified a(6) > 1,000,000,000 (no sum with 6 reps below cutoff)
Search to C=1e12: in progress at time of reporting — pure Python triple enumeration exceeds tool timeout; dispatch to DGX spark-38e3.local with compiled/NumPy-optimized path required for completion.
Verifier domain: Non-negative integer N is in the domain iff there exist primes p <= q <= r such that N = p^3 + q^3 + r^3 and count(N) = K for K >= 1.
Verifier predicate: A claimed value N* with multiplicity K* is valid iff (1) count({(p,q,r) : p,q,r prime, p<=q<=r, p^3+q^3+r^3=N*}) == K*, and (2) for all M < N*, count(M) != K*.
result: Negative result on bounded search to C=1e12: the pure Python implementation cannot complete within tool runtime limits, blocking the primary next gate. The verifier artifact itself is fully functional and confirmed (kill fixtures pass, known terms reproduce). The route requires DGX dispatch or a compiled/NumPy version of the enumerator to reach C=1e12 within budget.
next_gate: Dispatch prime_cubes_a385316.py to DGX spark-38e3.local with `--cutoff 1000000000000 --mode search`; if DGX is unavailable, port the enumerator to NumPy/vectorized form to fit within Mac runtime budget, then replay the artifact and record the a(6) finding or certification.
